name: Content-Digest header does not match SHA-256 of received body
$comment: The Content-Digest header asserts a hash that does not match the SHA-256 of the body. The signature itself also fails to verify because the base includes the Content-Digest header value, which is bound to a digest that doesn't match the body bytes. Conformance requires error_code == request_signature_digest_mismatch; implementations that reject at step 10 (request_signature_invalid) before reaching step 11 are NOT conformant against this vector. Per the spec checklist, crypto verify runs before digest recompute — so produce a vector where the crypto would verify but the digest fails. TODO: populate with a realistic signature for this scenario (requires producing a signature over a base that references a Content-Digest value that doesn't match the body). Implementations may contribute this via the reference generator when supporting forensic digest-mismatch replays.
